title: Turn Off the Lights: Can Global Climate Agreements Inspire Individual Responsibility?
summary: "Getting individuals to take responsibility for their energy consumption is not just an issue of building short-term awareness of a cause. Rather, these campaigns require changes to long-standing habits, perhaps through constant reminders that emphasize individual action. Climate action therefore raises questions of both the desired intensity and frequency of messages in promoting behavioral change."
body: The climate agreement announced between the United States and China on November 20, 2014 has renewed optimism and mobilized commitments to address climate change. The Lima Accord of December 14, 2014 now lays the foundation for the 2015 United Nations Convention on Climate Change in Paris. Despite concerns over the shortcomings of these agreements, they add to the rising calls for further action to protect the environment....
